feedback.text: Hei, As a British European I say its very important for unity and togetherness in alot of ways. Culture is not something that should be taken lightly and gaming is culture. It brings families closer, it brings people to new friends and it creates an environment of diversity and ethical acceptable due to your ability not to immediately be able to hate the people you talk to. It forces you to face your discriminations that are forced upon you by your local factors like neighbours, family, religion, culture and face the world at large. We need new laws or changes to existing laws that force games to stay available for future generations to experience as its a way for people of the next and following generations to understand and accept the people of today and think about more than just themselves. The EU is about being a family and changing the law around letting gaming companies kill games and forcing them to let games live, is a way for our big family to feel more connected.
